frameworks.name: hierarchical visual processing
frameworks.one_line: V1 extracts local borders; higher areas identify perceptually relevant ones

frameworks.name: feedforward vs feedback contextual modulation
frameworks.one_line: early feedforward V1 tuning plus delayed contextual enhancement for relevant borders

core_question: Does V1 distinguish perceptually relevant object borders from irrelevant ones in natural scenes?
key_assumptions: low-level features (contrast, oriented-filter energy) can be matched as controls
early V1 activity (~50 ms) reflects feedforward-driven tuning
manipulations: perceptually relevant vs irrelevant borders in natural images
response latency window (early feedforward vs delayed contextual)
key_findings: early visual cortex responds more strongly to perceptually relevant borders
discrimination emerges fast (~50 ms) during feedforward activity
an additional delayed contextual modulation further enhances responses to relevant borders
effect persists after matching simple low-level features
interpretations: V1 neurons are tuned to features characterizing perceptually relevant borders
border-relevance computation is not exclusive to higher areas; V1 carries both feedforward tuning and later contextual enhancement
